title: systemd generated file failed to start while it was working without systemd
body:
### Issue Description

I was not able to start a generated systemd unit for the container traefik and traefik-service while it was working without systemd.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I did a podman-compose up -d for the following docker-compose file after the command `systemctl --user enable --now podman.socket`.

```yaml
version: '3.8'

services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v2.10
    container_name: traefik
    command:
      # -  "--log.level=DEBUG"
      - "--api.insecure=true"
      - "--providers.docker=true"
      - "--providers.docker.exposedbydefault=false"
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.address=:443"
      - "--certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.dnschallenge=true"
      - "--certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.dnschallenge.provider=duckdns"
      # - "--certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.caserver=https://acme-staging-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory"
      - "--certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.email=admin@email.com"
      - "--certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.storage=/letsencrypt/acme.json"
    ports:
      - '443:443'
      - '5030:8080'  # (optional) expose the dashboard !don't use in production!
    environment:
      - "DUCKDNS_TOKEN_FILE=/run/secrets/secret"
    volumes:
      - './root/config:/etc/traefik'
      - './root/letsencrypt:/letsencrypt'
      - './root/logs:/var/log/traefik'
      - '/run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro'
    secrets:
      - secret
    networks:
      - 'traefik'

  whoami:
    image: "traefik/whoami"
    container_name: "traefik-service"
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.whoami.rule=Host(`whoami.example.com`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.whoami.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.whoami.tls.certresolver=myresolver"
    networks:
      - 'traefik'

secrets:
  secret:
    file: ./.secret

networks:
  traefik:
    name: 'traefik'
```


---

After podman-compose up both of the services are working fine and I was able to view my dashboard.
Then I wanted to enable this on startup so I created systemd files for them as follows.

---

```bash
podman generate systemd --new -n traefik > ~/.config/systemd/user/traefik.service
podman generate systemd --new -n traefik > ~/.config/systemd/user/traefik-service.service
systemctl --user daemon-reload
```



### Describe the results you received

Now when I enable try it one by one.

```bash
$ systemctl --user enable --now traefik-service
Created symlink /home/podman/.config/systemd/user/default.target.wants/traefik-service.service → /home/podman/.config/systemd/user/traefik-service.service.
Job for traefik-service.service failed because the control process exited with error code.
See "systemctl --user status traefik-service.service" and "journalctl --user -xeu traefik-service.service" for details.

$ systemctl --user enable --now traefik
Created symlink /home/podman/.config/systemd/user/default.target.wants/traefik.service → /home/podman/.config/systemd/user/traefik.service.
Job for traefik.service failed because the control process exited with error code.
See "systemctl --user status traefik.service" and "journalctl --user -xeu traefik.service" for details.
```

Now when I check `podman ps -a`. Container traefik is missing while traefik-service is running. However both system units failed.

Generated system-units are as follows:

**Service traefik**

```bash
# container-traefik.service
# autogenerated by Podman 4.3.1
# Tue Jul  4 12:31:20 +0530 2023

[Unit]
Description=Podman container-traefik.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=/bin/rm \
        -f %t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run \
        --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id \
        --cgroups=no-conmon \
        --rm \
        --sdnotify=conmon \
        --replace \
        --name=traefik \
        -d \
        --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 \
        --label io.podman.compose.project=traefik \
        --label io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 \
        --label com.docker.compose.project=traefik \
        --label com.docker.compose.project.working_dir=/home/podman/.opt/podman/traefik \
        --label com.docker.compose.project.config_files=docker-compose.yml \
        --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 \
        --label com.docker.compose.service=traefik \
        -e DUCKDNS_TOKEN_FILE=/run/secrets/scr \
        -v /home/podman/.opt/podman/traefik/root/config:/etc/traefik \
        -v /home/podman/.opt/podman/traefik/root/letsencrypt:/letsencrypt \
        -v /home/podman/.opt/podman/traefik/root/logs:/var/log/traefik \
        -v /run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro \
        --net traefik \
        --network-alias traefik \
        --volume ./.scr:/run/secrets/scr:ro,rprivate,rbind \
        -p 443:443 \
        -p 33693:8080 traefik:v2.10 \
        --api.insecure=true \
        --providers.docker=true \
        --providers.docker.exposedbydefault=false \
        --entrypoints.web.address=:80 \
        --entrypoints.websecure.address=:443 \
        --certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.dnschallenge=true \
        --certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.dnschallenge.provider=duckdns \
        --certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.email=contabo.arzdq@aleeas.com \
        --certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.storage=/letsencrypt/acme.json
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop \
        --ignore -t 10 \
        --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm \
        -f \
        --ignore -t 10 \
        --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

**Service traefik-service**

```
# container-traefik.service
# autogenerated by Podman 4.3.1
# Tue Jul  4 12:31:20 +0530 2023

[Unit]
Description=Podman container-traefik.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=/bin/rm \
        -f %t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run \
        --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id \
        --cgroups=no-conmon \
        --rm \
        --sdnotify=conmon \
        --replace \
        --name=traefik \
        -d \
        --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 \
        --label io.podman.compose.project=traefik \
        --label io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 \
        --label com.docker.compose.project=traefik \
        --label com.docker.compose.project.working_dir=/home/podman/.opt/podman/traefik \
        --label com.docker.compose.project.config_files=docker-compose.yml \
        --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 \
        --label com.docker.compose.service=traefik \
        -e DUCKDNS_TOKEN_FILE=/run/secrets/scr \
        -v /home/podman/.opt/podman/traefik/root/config:/etc/traefik \
        -v /home/podman/.opt/podman/traefik/root/letsencrypt:/letsencrypt \
        -v /home/podman/.opt/podman/traefik/root/logs:/var/log/traefik \
        -v /run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro \
        --net traefik \
        --network-alias traefik \
        --volume ./.scr:/run/secrets/scr:ro,rprivate,rbind \
        -p 443:443 \
        -p 33693:8080 traefik:v2.10 \
        --api.insecure=true \
        --providers.docker=true \
        --providers.docker.exposedbydefault=false \
        --entrypoints.web.address=:80 \
        --entrypoints.websecure.address=:443 \
        --certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.dnschallenge=true \
        --certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.dnschallenge.provider=duckdns \
        --certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.email=contabo.arzdq@aleeas.com \
        --certificatesresolvers.myresolver.acme.storage=/letsencrypt/acme.json
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop \
        --ignore -t 10 \
        --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm \
        -f \
        --ignore -t 10 \
        --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

I also would like to create both of this containers in one single pod defining for podman-compose but I did not find a way so that I can start both the containers with the same pod as I have mentioned [here](https://github.com/containers/podman-compose/issues/720).



### Describe the results you expected

I was expecting to run this these services on startup using systemd.

### podman info output

```yaml

  kernel: 6.1.0-9-amd64

      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    exists: true

  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

I am running Debian 12 on a Virtualbox.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @zak-admin.

Do you find anything useful in the logs of services? `journalctl --user -xeu traefik.service`?

@vrothberg I did not find anything useful.

```bash
Jul 05 13:16:52 whoami systemd[626]: traefik.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
░░ Subject: Unit failed
░░ Defined-By: systemd
░░ Support: https://www.debian.org/support
░░
░░ The unit UNIT has entered the 'failed' state with result 'exit-code'.
Jul 05 13:16:52 whoami systemd[626]: Failed to start traefik.service - Podman container-traefik.service.
░░ Subject: A start job for unit UNIT has failed
░░ Defined-By: systemd
░░ Support: https://www.debian.org/support
░░
░░ A start job for unit UNIT has finished with a failure.
░░
░░ The job identifier is 133 and the job result is failed.
Jul 05 13:16:52 whoami systemd[626]: traefik.service: Scheduled restart job, restart counter is at 5.
░░ Subject: Automatic restarting of a unit has been scheduled
░░ Defined-By: systemd
░░ Support: https://www.debian.org/support
░░
░░ Automatic restarting of the unit UNIT has been scheduled, as the result for
░░ the configured Restart= setting for the unit.
Jul 05 13:16:52 whoami systemd[626]: Stopped traefik.service - Podman container-traefik.service.
░░ Subject: A stop job for unit UNIT has finished
░░ Defined-By: systemd
░░ Support: https://www.debian.org/support
░░
░░ A stop job for unit UNIT has finished.
░░
░░ The job identifier is 163 and the job result is done.
Jul 05 13:16:52 whoami systemd[626]: traefik.service: Start request repeated too quickly.
Jul 05 13:16:52 vwhoami systemd[626]: traefik.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
░░ Subject: Unit failed
░░ Defined-By: systemd
░░ Support: https://www.debian.org/support
░░
░░ The unit UNIT has entered the 'failed' state with result 'exit-code'.
Jul 05 13:16:52 whoami systemd[626]: Failed to start traefik.service - Podman container-traefik.service.
░░ Subject: A start job for unit UNIT has failed
░░ Defined-By: systemd
░░ Support: https://www.debian.org/support
░░
░░ A start job for unit UNIT has finished with a failure.
░░
░░ The job identifier is 163 and the job result is failed.
```

@zak-admin You need to scroll up until you see an actual error Podman message.

> podman generate systemd --new -n traefik > ~/.config/systemd/user/traefik.service
podman generate systemd --new -n traefik > ~/.config/systemd/user/traefik-service.service
systemctl --user daemon-reload

Is this a typo or your real commands? You generate the same container twice thus the units are identical and this will not work due naming and port conflicts.

Thank you for spotting it. It was not a typo. It is my mistake. Anyway after creating the correct configuration file for traefik-service. traefik-service.service works. But yet traefik.service does not work.

> @zak-admin You need to scroll up until you see an actual error Podman message.

This is still valid, we need to see the actual error in order to help you, does not work is not something we can work with.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since I have not heard anything back, closing.
